description: A text and quote card (page 1 of a multi-image set) carrying a DHS, FBI, FAA and DoD joint statement. The layout is on a solid DHS-blue background with red horizontal rules; a large blue-and-white circular quotation-mark icon sits in the top-left corner. A centered bold heading reads 'DHS, FBI, FAA & DoD Joint Statement on Ongoing Response to Reported Drone Sightings'. Below, in white body text, are three paragraphs of the statement: that more than one million drones are registered with the FAA and thousands of commercial, hobbyist and law-enforcement drones fly daily; that the FBI received tips of more than 5,000 reported drone sightings in recent weeks with about 100 leads generated, and that detection technology and trained visual observers were deployed; and an assessment that the sightings to date were a combination of commercial drones, hobbyist drones, law-enforcement drones, manned fixed-wing aircraft, helicopters, and stars reported as drones, with nothing anomalous identified and no national-security or public-safety risk assessed over civilian airspace in New Jersey or the northeast. The DHS seal appears at the bottom right.
notable_text: DHS, FBI, FAA & DoD Joint Statement on Ongoing Response to Reported Drone Sightings; 'We have not identified anything anomalous and do not assess the activity to date to present a national security or public safety risk'
